labRegulatoryExposures.rationale: EU-facing GPAI or AI-system offerings can trigger EU AI Act obligations even when the lab is headquartered outside the EU.
labRegulatoryExposures.notes: This is not an official lab-specific enforcement finding; it is a legal-applicability hook based on provider activity in the EU market.
labRegulatoryExposures.verificationNotes: Official source verifies the EU AI Act and its GPAI obligations. Lab-specific exposure is assessed from EU market-facing activity, not from a lab-named enforcement record.

internationalInstruments.name: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
internationalInstruments.summary: The first global standard-setting instrument on AI ethics. Sets universal principles on human rights, dignity, fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy, oversight, and sustainability. Adopted by UNESCO's General Conference on 23 November 2021; broadly cited by national regulators and regional bodies.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: UNESCO — UNESCO adopts first global standard on the ethics of AI
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official UNESCO source confirms adoption on 23 November 2021. Avoid hard-coding a member-state count because UNESCO membership differs from the map's UN-country coverage.

internationalInstruments.name: UN General Assembly Resolution 78/265 "Safe, secure and trustworthy AI systems for sustainable development"
internationalInstruments.summary: First broad UNGA resolution focused on safe, secure and trustworthy AI, reaffirming international law and framing AI through sustainable development and human rights. Adopted by consensus.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: UN Digital Library record confirms A/RES/78/265 and date 2024-03-21.

internationalInstruments.name: UN General Assembly Resolution 78/311 "Enhancing international cooperation on capacity-building of AI"
internationalInstruments.summary: Focuses on AI capacity-building, digital divides, infrastructure, skills, and developing-country participation in AI governance.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: UN Digital Library record confirms A/RES/78/311 and date 2024-07-01.

internationalInstruments.name: Global Digital Compact (Pact for the Future)
internationalInstruments.summary: First UN comprehensive framework for digital cooperation and AI governance. Commits states to UN-level mechanisms for AI governance dialogue, risk mitigation, human oversight, and capacity-building. Negotiated by 193 UN member states.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official UN Global Digital Compact page. Participation remains represented via UN membership coverage, not individual signature.

internationalInstruments.name: UN General Assembly Resolution 79/325 — Independent International Scientific Panel on AI and Global Dialogue on AI Governance
internationalInstruments.summary: Formally establishes a 40-member independent scientific panel and a global dialogue process on AI governance. Most concrete institutional step the UN has taken toward standing AI governance machinery.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: UN sources confirm A/RES/79/325 was adopted on 2025-08-26.

internationalInstruments.name: OECD Recommendation on AI / OECD AI Principles
internationalInstruments.summary: Most influential intergovernmental AI policy instrument, setting principles on inclusive growth, human rights, transparency, robustness, and accountability. Revised in May 2024 to reflect generative AI. Adhered to by 38 OECD members + EU + 8 non-member adherents.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: OECD Legal Instruments record confirms adoption on 22 May 2019 and revisions including 3 May 2024.

internationalInstruments.name: G20 AI Principles
internationalInstruments.issuer: G20 Trade and Digital Economy Ministers / G20 Leaders
internationalInstruments.summary: G20 AI-principles annex to the Ministerial Statement on Trade and Digital Economy. Effectively internationalized the OECD approach across major economies.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: OECD source confirms the G20 AI Principles drew from the OECD Recommendation; original G20 host should be checked in a later pass.

internationalInstruments.name: Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence declarations and ministerial statements
internationalInstruments.summary: Series of GPAI Council outcome documents giving ministerial-level direction to the Global Partnership on Artificial Intelligence work programme. Included as AI policy coordination context, not as a treaty or binding legal instrument.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official OECD.AI page lists GPAI Council outcome documents from November 2022, December 2023, July 2024, December 2024, and February 2026. Date is normalized to the first listed month for timeline placement.
internationalInstruments.notes: No country participation rows are included until a canonical current member/signatory roster is verified from official GPAI/OECD sources.

internationalInstruments.name: G7 Leaders' Statement on the Hiroshima AI Process
internationalInstruments.summary: Political umbrella for the G7 frontier-AI governance package. Explicitly addresses advanced AI systems including foundation models and generative AI, and endorses the follow-on Guiding Principles and Code of Conduct.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: Japan MOFA — Hiroshima AI Process Statement

internationalInstruments.name: Hiroshima Process International Guiding Principles for Organizations Developing Advanced AI Systems
internationalInstruments.summary: One of the clearest international texts aimed directly at frontier-model developers. Covers risk identification, red-teaming, incident reporting, cybersecurity, watermarking/authentication, public reporting, and post-deployment monitoring.

internationalInstruments.name: Hiroshima Process International Code of Conduct for Organizations Developing Advanced AI Systems
internationalInstruments.summary: Translates the Hiroshima principles into concrete organizational actions on governance, safety testing, incident handling, transparency, model-weight security, content provenance, and information-sharing.

internationalInstruments.name: Hiroshima AI Process Reporting Framework
internationalInstruments.summary: Operationalizes the Hiroshima Code by giving companies a structured way to disclose governance, risk-management, incident handling, and safety practices for advanced AI systems.

internationalInstruments.name: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689)
internationalInstruments.issuer: European Parliament and Council of the EU
internationalInstruments.summary: First binding, risk-based AI law including provisions on general-purpose AI models. In force from 1 August 2024 with a phased implementation timeline. Has extraterritorial commercial effect.
internationalInstruments.notes: Applies to all 27 EU member states via 'applicable_via_eu' participation type, plus the EU itself as the regulating authority.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: European Commission and EUR-Lex sources confirm Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 entered into force on 2024-08-01 with phased application.

internationalInstruments.name: Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI and Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law
internationalInstruments.summary: First international legally binding treaty on AI. Requires that the AI lifecycle be compatible with human rights, democracy and the rule of law; mandates risk and impact assessments, remedies, transparency, and a follow-up mechanism. Adopted 17 May 2024; opened for signature 5 September 2024. As of 19 May 2026, not yet in force (needs five ratifications including at least three Council of Europe member states). The EU ratified on 15 May 2026.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: Council of Europe — Framework Convention / Treaty Office
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Council of Europe Treaty Office/news confirms EU ratification on 2026-05-15. Treaty Office summaries confirm entry into force requires five ratifications including at least three Council of Europe member states.

internationalInstruments.name: CEN-CENELEC AI Act harmonized-standardization work
internationalInstruments.summary: European standardization workstream developing harmonized standards in support of the EU AI Act, including risk management, quality management, conformity assessment, transparency, human oversight, cybersecurity, and related operational topics.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official CEN-CENELEC page states that JTC 21 was established on 2021-06-01 and develops European AI standards, including harmonized standards supporting the EU AI Act.
internationalInstruments.notes: Standardization infrastructure, not a standalone binding law. Harmonized standards may support legal presumption of conformity once published in the Official Journal of the EU.

internationalInstruments.name: Council of the EU conclusions on strengthening the EU's ambitions in AI
internationalInstruments.summary: Council conclusions calling for stronger EU leadership in trustworthy and human-centric AI, international standards work, talent, innovation, and global governance outreach. Soft law / political guidance.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Council press release confirms approval of conclusions on 2024-11-05; this is political guidance, not binding law.

internationalInstruments.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Artificial intelligence — Management system
internationalInstruments.summary: World's first AI management-system standard. Establishes requirements for organizations to govern AI across the lifecycle. Widely used in audits, procurement, and enterprise assurance.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official ISO page confirms ISO/IEC 42001:2023, publication 2023-12, and management-system scope.

internationalInstruments.name: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — AI — Guidance on risk management
internationalInstruments.summary: Voluntary international standard giving organizations a structured way to manage AI-specific risks across development, deployment, and use.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official ISO page confirms ISO/IEC 23894:2023 and publication 2023-02.

internationalInstruments.name: ISO/IEC 38507:2022 — Governance implications of the use of AI by organizations
internationalInstruments.summary: Board-level governance standard explaining the governance implications of AI use by organizations.

internationalInstruments.name: ISO/IEC 22989:2022 — AI concepts and terminology
internationalInstruments.summary: Foundational terminology standard for AI. Shapes interoperability of subsequent standards, procurement language, and policy drafting.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official ISO page confirms ISO/IEC 22989:2022 and publication 2022-07.

internationalInstruments.name: ISO/IEC 42005:2025 — AI system impact assessment
internationalInstruments.summary: Standard focused on identifying and documenting AI system impacts on individuals, groups, and society.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official ISO page confirms ISO/IEC 42005:2025 and International Standard publication on 2025-05-28.

internationalInstruments.name: Bletchley Declaration
internationalInstruments.summary: First major international declaration centred on frontier-AI safety risks. Legitimised frontier-AI risk as an object of international coordination and triggered the State of the Science work and AI Safety Institute track. New Zealand later joined the participants.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: GOV.UK page lists represented countries and states that New Zealand joined the commitment on 2024-10-23.

internationalInstruments.name: Seoul Declaration for safe, innovative and inclusive AI
internationalInstruments.summary: Continues the Bletchley process and broadens it beyond safety to include innovation and inclusivity. Links frontier-model safety to democratic values, privacy, human rights, and bridging AI divides.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: GOV.UK leaders-session page confirms participating governments for the Seoul Declaration.

internationalInstruments.name: Seoul Statement of Intent toward International Cooperation on AI Safety Science
internationalInstruments.summary: Annex to the Seoul Declaration that formalises cooperation on AI safety science and acknowledges the role of state-supported AI Safety Institutes.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: GOV.UK page publishes the statement of intent as part of the AI Seoul Summit 2024 materials.

internationalInstruments.name: Seoul Ministerial Statement for advancing AI safety, innovation and inclusivity
internationalInstruments.summary: More operational than the leaders' Seoul Declaration. Translates the summit vision into practical steps on governance, collaboration, safety, and sustainable development. Broader geographic participation.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: GOV.UK ministerial-statement page confirms signatories and date 2024-05-22.

internationalInstruments.name: Frontier AI Safety Commitments (AI Seoul Summit 2024)
internationalInstruments.issuer: UK and Republic of Korea governments / participating frontier AI companies
internationalInstruments.summary: Voluntary company commitments announced in the AI Seoul Summit process. Participating organizations undertake to manage severe frontier-AI risks, publish safety frameworks, use risk thresholds, explain mitigations, and provide transparency where possible.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official GOV.UK page lists participating companies and describes the commitments as voluntary. The app links only companies already represented as frontier labs.
internationalInstruments.notes: Non-state company commitments, not an intergovernmental treaty, national law, or state sign-on. No country participation rows are included.

internationalInstruments.name: Statement on Inclusive and Sustainable AI for People and the Planet
internationalInstruments.summary: Adopted at the Paris AI Action Summit. Shifts focus from pure safety to public-interest AI — access, openness, sustainability, labour, and environment. The official signatory list includes 63 countries plus the African Union Commission and the EU; the United States and United Kingdom do not appear on that list.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: Élysée — Statement on Inclusive and Sustainable AI
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official Élysée page provides the signatory list. The app maps the EU but not the African Union Commission as a separate country row.

internationalInstruments.name: International Network of AI Safety Institutes (now International Network for Advanced AI Measurement, Evaluation and Science)
internationalInstruments.issuer: Governments participating in the Seoul process
internationalInstruments.summary: Intergovernmental network for AI safety science, evaluation methods, and joint research. Launched by 10 countries plus the EU; refocused and renamed on 9 December 2025.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: NIST — Mission Statement of the International Network of AISIs
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: GOV.UK source confirms launch members; NIST source confirms the mission statement. Participation rows use the GOV.UK launch-member source.

internationalInstruments.name: ASEAN Guide on AI Governance and Ethics
internationalInstruments.summary: Regional, interoperability-oriented governance toolkit for trustworthy commercial AI in non-military settings. Risk-based governance, human oversight, explainability, incident management, and internal governance structures.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: ASEAN — Guide on AI Governance and Ethics

internationalInstruments.name: Expanded ASEAN Guide on AI Governance and Ethics — Generative AI
internationalInstruments.summary: Extends the 2024 ASEAN guide to generative AI. Covers synthetic content, model risk, misuse, transparency, copyright, bias, privacy, and operational governance.

internationalInstruments.name: African Union Continental AI Strategy
internationalInstruments.summary: Continental, development-focused AI strategy emphasising digital sovereignty, infrastructure, data, skills, innovation ecosystems, inclusive growth, and African representation in global AI governance.

internationalInstruments.name: APEC Digital and AI Ministerial Statement
internationalInstruments.summary: Links AI governance to digital trade, interoperability, standards, capability-building, and safe AI use across the Asia-Pacific.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: APEC — Digital and AI Ministerial Statement

internationalInstruments.name: APEC AI Initiative 2026–2030
internationalInstruments.summary: Five-year regional framework on AI innovation, governance, talent, interoperability, and diffusion. Connects AI governance to infrastructure and economic competitiveness.

internationalInstruments.name: EU–U.S. Joint Roadmap on Evaluation and Measurement Tools for Trustworthy AI and Risk Management
internationalInstruments.summary: Bilateral roadmap to align EU and U.S. work on AI evaluation and measurement tools, terminology, taxonomies, and risk management approaches.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: European Commission — TTC Joint AI Roadmap

internationalInstruments.name: UK–U.S. Memorandum of Understanding on AI safety
internationalInstruments.issuer: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology / U.S. Department of Commerce
internationalInstruments.summary: Bilateral MoU between the UK and U.S. AI Safety Institutes (AISI / CAISI) on cooperation, joint evaluations, methods, personnel exchanges, and information-sharing.

internationalInstruments.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
internationalInstruments.issuer: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology
internationalInstruments.summary: Cross-sectoral companion profile to NIST AI RMF 1.0 for generative AI. Gives organizations voluntary guidance for identifying, measuring, managing, and governing generative-AI risks.
internationalInstruments.sourceName: NIST - AI RMF Generative AI Profile
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Official NIST publication page confirms publication on 2024-07-26, report number NIST AI 600-1, and voluntary AI RMF companion-profile scope.
internationalInstruments.notes: U.S. technical guidance with international influence. It is not an international agreement and has no country participation rows.

internationalInstruments.name: Frontier Model Forum
internationalInstruments.issuer: Founding companies: Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, OpenAI
internationalInstruments.summary: Industry forum coordinating frontier AI safety, security, thresholds, evaluations, and information-sharing among leading frontier-model companies. Not an intergovernmental instrument — included here to make the corporate-governance layer visible.
internationalInstruments.verificationNotes: Issuer-controlled non-state source. Treat as industry-governance context, not intergovernmental law.
internationalInstruments.notes: Non-state / industry governance. No per-country participation rows are included on the map because the Forum is not signed by states.

internationalParticipation.sourceName: European Commission — TTC Joint AI Roadmap
European Commission — TTC Joint AI Roadmap

nationalAIRegulations.name: EU AI Act
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: European Commission AI Office and national supervisory authorities
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Binding, risk-based AI regulation, including provisions on general-purpose AI models. In force across all 27 EU member states with a phased implementation timeline. Has extraterritorial commercial effect on providers serving the EU market.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: EUR-Lex — Regulation 2024/1689
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: EUR-Lex and European Commission sources confirm in-force date 2024-08-01 and phased application. EU member-state coverage is applicability via EU law, not national enactment.

nationalAIRegulations.name: European AI Office
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: European Commission
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Implementing and governance structure for the EU AI Act, with a particular focus on general-purpose AI models, codes of practice, and coordination with national authorities.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: European Commission — AI Office
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official Commission source confirms the AI Office's role. It is an institutional framework, not itself a binding AI rule; binding obligations come from the EU AI Act.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) with MIIT, MPS, and others
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Binding rules for public-facing generative AI services in China. Cover content moderation, training-data requirements, security assessment, and filing obligations.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: CAC — Interim Measures for Generative AI Services
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official CAC source confirms the generative-AI service measures, promulgation on 2023-07-13, and 2023-08-15 effective date.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Provisions on the Administration of Deep Synthesis Internet Information Services
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: CAC, MIIT, MPS
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Binding rules on deep-synthesis services, including labelling of synthetic content, prohibited uses, security assessments, and provider obligations.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: CAC — Deep Synthesis provisions
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official CAC source confirms the deep-synthesis provisions, 2022-11-25 adoption, and 2023-01-10 effective date.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Provisions on the Administration of Algorithmic Recommendation Services for Internet Information Services
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: CAC, MIIT, MPS, SAMR
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Binding rules on recommendation algorithms in internet services. Imposes transparency, user control, content-moderation, and security-assessment obligations.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: CAC — Algorithmic Recommendation provisions
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official CAC source confirms the algorithmic-recommendation provisions and 2022-03-01 effective date.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Measures for the Labeling of AI-Generated Synthetic Content
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: CAC, MIIT, MPS, NRTA
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Binding requirements for labelling AI-generated synthetic content (text, image, audio, video) with both explicit user-visible markings and embedded metadata.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: CAC — AI Content Labeling Measures
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official CAC notice confirms the AI-generated synthetic-content labeling measures and the 2025-09-01 effective date.

nationalAIRegulations.name: AI Basic Act (Framework Act on the Development of AI and Foundational Trust)
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Science and ICT (MSIT); National AI Committee; AI Safety Institute
nationalAIRegulations.summary: First Korean horizontal AI statute. Covers high-impact AI, transparency duties, domestic representative requirements, and an institutional safety ecosystem. Phased implementation with a grace period.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: MSIT — AI Basic Act explanation
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official MSIT source confirms the Act passed the National Assembly on 2024-12-26 and is scheduled to take effect on 2026-01-22.

nationalAIRegulations.name: AI regulation: a pro-innovation approach (white paper response)
nationalAIRegulations.status: In force as guidance; no omnibus AI statute
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: DSIT, with sectoral regulators (ICO, CMA, FCA, MHRA, Ofcom, HSE)
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Cross-cutting principles approach to AI regulation in the UK, implemented through existing sectoral regulators rather than a single AI act.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: GOV.UK — pro-innovation approach response

nationalAIRegulations.name: UK AI Security Institute (AISI)
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
nationalAIRegulations.summary: First state-supported institute focused on frontier AI safety evaluations. Conducts pre-deployment testing and produces public research on capability and safety.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: UK AISI — About

nationalAIRegulations.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Voluntary risk-management framework for AI. Widely used inside and outside the U.S. as a baseline reference; the 2024 Generative AI Profile is tracked separately as an operational instrument.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: NIST — AI Risk Management Framework
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official NIST page confirms AI RMF 1.0 publication on 2023-01-26; framework is voluntary/non-binding. The Generative AI Profile is represented separately as nist-genai-profile.

nationalAIRegulations.name: U.S. Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI / U.S. AI Safety Institute)
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: NIST / U.S. Department of Commerce
nationalAIRegulations.summary: U.S. body responsible for AI safety/security science, evaluations, and international cooperation through the global AISI network.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: NIST — CAISI
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official NIST CAISI page confirms institutional role; it is not itself a binding legal instrument.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA) — proposed in Bill C-27
nationalAIRegulations.status: Historical proposal in Bill C-27; not enacted and not in force
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED)
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Proposed federal AI statute focused on high-impact AI systems, risk management, and oversight. Bill C-27 did not become law; no federal AIDA is in force as of the May 2026 snapshot.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: ISED — Artificial Intelligence and Data Act
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: ISED describes AIDA as part of Bill C-27. Parliament status indicates Bill C-27 did not enact; keep as historical/proposed context, not an active bill or law.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Voluntary Code of Conduct on the Responsible Development and Management of Advanced Generative AI Systems
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: ISED
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Voluntary code addressing safety, fairness, transparency, accountability, robustness, and human oversight for advanced generative AI systems.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: ISED — Voluntary Code of Conduct on Advanced Generative AI

nationalAIRegulations.name: Canadian AI Safety Institute (CAISI)
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: ISED
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Canadian institute focused on frontier-AI safety research, evaluations, and international cooperation.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: ISED — Canadian Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute

nationalAIRegulations.name: Act on the Promotion of Research, Development and Utilisation of AI-Related Technologies
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Cabinet Secretariat / Cabinet Office
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Promotion-focused statute establishing the basic principles, government plan, AI Strategy HQ, and obligations for the development and use of AI-related technologies in Japan.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Japanese Law Translation — AI-related Promotion Law
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Japanese Law Translation identifies Act No. 53 of 2025, promulgated/enforced 2025-06-04 except specified provisions.

nationalAIRegulations.name: AI Guidelines for Business
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry (METI) and Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications (MIC)
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Cross-cutting business-facing guidelines covering developer, provider, and user duties for AI systems including generative AI.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: METI — AI Guidelines for Business

nationalAIRegulations.name: Japan AI Safety Institute
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: IPA (Information-technology Promotion Agency), Cabinet Office
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Japanese national AI safety institute responsible for safety evaluation, guidelines, and international cooperation.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Japan AISI — About AISI
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official AISI page confirms establishment on 2024-02-14 and its role in AI safety evaluation, guidance, and international cooperation.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Model AI Governance Framework for Generative AI
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA); AI Verify Foundation
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Voluntary governance framework for generative AI in Singapore. Covers accountability, data, trusted development, incident reporting, testing, safety, content provenance, and AI for public good.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: IMDA — Model AI Governance Framework for GenAI

nationalAIRegulations.name: AI Verify
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: AI Verify Foundation; IMDA
nationalAIRegulations.summary: AI governance testing framework and software toolkit allowing organisations to test their AI systems against internationally recognised AI principles.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: IMDA — AI Verify Foundation

nationalAIRegulations.name: Voluntary AI Safety Standard
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Department of Industry, Science and Resources
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Ten voluntary guardrails aligned with international AI standards, focused on accountability, risk management, data governance, testing, transparency, and human oversight.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Department of Industry, Science and Resources — Voluntary AI Safety Standard

nationalAIRegulations.name: Proposed mandatory guardrails for AI in high-risk settings
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Department of Industry, Science and Resources
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Government proposal for mandatory guardrails on the development and deployment of AI in high-risk settings, building on the voluntary AI Safety Standard.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Department of Industry, Science and Resources — Proposed mandatory guardrails

nationalAIRegulations.name: IndiaAI Mission
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
nationalAIRegulations.summary: National AI mission with components on compute, datasets, foundation models, applications, skilling, startup financing, and safe/trusted AI. India's main AI-specific policy programme.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: IndiaAI — Official portal

nationalAIRegulations.name: Law of the Republic of Kazakhstan On Artificial Intelligence
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of AI and Digital Development; AI Development Council
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Comprehensive horizontal AI statute with high/medium/low risk categories. Prohibits AI for behavioural manipulation, social scoring, and certain biometric classifications; sets transparency, security, and accountability duties for owners, possessors, and users of AI systems.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Kazakhstan Adilet — Law On Artificial Intelligence
nationalAIRegulations.notes: Accompanied by amendments to existing laws to support AI governance.
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official Adilet legal database record confirms the Law of the Republic of Kazakhstan dated 2025-11-17 No. 230-VIII ZRK, titled 'On Artificial Intelligence', with Article 31 providing entry into force after official publication.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Law on Artificial Intelligence (Vietnam)
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Science and Technology
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Risk-based AI statute with tiers including prohibited AI uses. Requires human oversight, fairness, transparency, non-bias and accountability. Providers self-classify risk and notify MoST for medium/high risk systems; high-risk systems require registration, risk assessments, and sample audits.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Vietnam Government Portal - Law 134/2025/QH15
nationalAIRegulations.notes: Builds on and supersedes the risk-based framework in the Law on Digital Technology Industry (June 2025).
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Government legal database record confirms Law 134/2025/QH15, promulgation date 2025-12-10, and effective date 2026-03-01.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Law on Digital Technology Industry
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Information and Communications
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Earlier risk-based framework on the digital technology industry that includes AI provisions. Largely superseded by the Law on AI for AI-specific obligations.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Vietnam Government Portal - Law 71/2025/QH15
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Government legal database record confirms Law 71/2025/QH15, promulgation date 2025-06-14, and general effective date 2026-01-01; selected provisions entered into force earlier.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Artificial Intelligence Basic Act (Taiwan)
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Digital Affairs; National Science and Technology Council
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Foundational statute providing Taiwan's human-centered AI governance framework. Consolidates the AI Action Plan 2.0 priorities (talent, software and chips, international links, regulatory environment, industrial transformation) into binding form.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Taiwan NSTC - Artificial Intelligence Basic Act
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: MODA confirms Legislative Yuan passage on 2025-12-23; NSTC legal database confirms promulgation on 2026-01-14 and provides the full act text.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Law No. 132/2025 — Provisions and delegations to the Government on artificial intelligence
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: National Cybersecurity Agency (ACN); Agency for Digital Italy (AGID)
nationalAIRegulations.summary: First European national law implementing the EU AI Act. Designates ACN and AGID as competent national authorities for monitoring and inspecting AI systems, and provides for stable interinstitutional coordination on AI compliance.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Gazzetta Ufficiale — Law 132/2025
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Gazzetta Ufficiale ELI metadata confirms Law 132/2025, document date 2025-09-23, publication date 2025-09-25, title on artificial intelligence, and entry into force on 2025-10-10.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Law on the Implementation of the EU AI Act (Slovenia)
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Digital Transformation
nationalAIRegulations.summary: National implementation of the EU AI Act. Mandates public-sector publication of high-risk AI systems and establishes regulatory sandboxes under supervised conditions.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: PISRS - ZIUDHPUI
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: PISRS record confirms adoption on 2025-10-23, publication in Official Gazette RS 85/25, and validity/application from 2025-11-21.

nationalAIRegulations.name: TAKE IT DOWN Act
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Federal Trade Commission
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Federal statute criminalising the nonconsensual publication of intimate images, including AI-generated 'digital forgeries' (deepfakes). Requires covered online platforms to implement notice-and-removal processes for intimate images at the depicted individual's request. Included as an AI-specific content/deepfake law, not as broad frontier-model governance.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: U.S. Congress — Public Law 119-12 (TAKE IT DOWN Act)
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Congress.gov verifies enactment as Public Law 119-12. Inclusion is narrower than frontier AI governance; treat as a content/deepfake statute.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Seventh Five-Year Development Plan Law — Article 65(c) (AI provisions)
nationalAIRegulations.status: In force as a broad development-plan mandate; not a standalone AI law
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: AI Task Force; Supreme Council of Cyberspace
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Broad development-plan mandate requiring the Government to implement a National AI Development Programme, including legal, technical, ethical and social infrastructure. Kept as AI-governance context, not as a standalone binding AI-specific regulatory law for map coloring.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Islamic Consultative Assembly — Seventh Five-Year Development Plan
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official Majlis source is protected by an anti-bot challenge during automated review; source search snippets and prior review support an AI programme mandate in Article 65(c). Kept as non-binding governance context and excluded from binding-law map coloring.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Federal Law No. 123-FZ on the Experimental Legal Regime for AI in Moscow
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Government of the Russian Federation
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Experimental legal regime allowing the testing of AI technologies in the city of Moscow for five years. Later amended to restrict participation by foreign-owned firms.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Russia — Federal Law 123-FZ
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official publication portal confirms Federal Law No. 123-FZ dated and published 2020-04-24 on the AI experimental legal regime in Moscow.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Proposed Law Regulating Artificial Intelligence Technologies and Their Uses
nationalAIRegulations.status: Shura Council proposal accepted for government drafting
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Shura Council; Government of Bahrain

nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Bahrain Shura Council — Annual Report 2023–2024
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official Shura Council annual report lists the AI technologies and uses proposal, 2024-04-28 session discussion, and decision to refer it to the Government for drafting. It does not confirm enactment.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Bill 2338/2023 — Legal Framework for AI
nationalAIRegulations.status: Approved by Senate; under consideration in Chamber of Deputies
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Federal Senate; Chamber of Deputies (Câmara dos Deputados)
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Comprehensive risk-based AI bill establishing rights for affected persons, governance duties for developers and operators, and supervisory authority. Builds on the earlier 21/2020 draft after civil-society criticism over algorithmic discrimination.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Federal Senate of Brazil — PL 2338/2023
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official Senate matter page supports PL 2338/2023 as an AI framework bill; keep proposed unless a Chamber enactment or official gazette source is added.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Law for the Responsible Promotion of Artificial Intelligence in Costa Rica (Bill 23919)

nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Legislative Assembly of Costa Rica

nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Costa Rica Legislative Assembly — Expediente 23919
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official Legislative Assembly project page supports Expediente 23919 as an AI-related bill. Status details may change and should remain proposed until plenary enactment is verified.

nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official TBMM bill page confirms the proposal record. Keep as proposed; later commission or enactment status needs a fresh Turkish-language official-source review.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Bill regulating AI Systems and their Applications in the Dominican Republic

nationalAIRegulations.name: Federal Law Regulating Artificial Intelligence (Mexico)

nationalAIRegulations.name: Artificial Intelligence (Regulation) Bill — relaunched by Lord Holmes
nationalAIRegulations.status: Passed first reading in House of Lords
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: House of Lords
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Private member's bill setting out five principles of AI regulation (safety, transparency, fairness, accountability, contestability) and proposing a central AI Authority. Relaunched in 2025 after the 2023 version lapsed.

subnationalAIRules.name: France - EU AI Act implementation activity
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: Official CNIL annual-report source supports French EU AI Act implementation activity and CNIL's AI-governance role. The record has been softened because this source does not establish a comprehensive national implementation act.
subnationalAIRules.status: Implementation activity; specific national act not tracked
subnationalAIRules.summary: French EU AI Act implementation activity, including CNIL's AI governance and supervisory work. This entry is not treated as a national AI Act or binding implementation law until a specific official act is added.

subnationalAIRules.name: Germany - draft EU AI Act implementation
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: Official Bundestag release supports a 2025 draft implementation bill and BNetzA's proposed central role. Keep as proposed/draft until the act is promulgated.
subnationalAIRules.status: Draft; BNetzA proposed as central market surveillance authority
subnationalAIRules.summary: German implementation of the EU AI Act. BNetzA (Federal Network Agency) proposed as central market surveillance authority; sectoral coordination with BSI and BaFin.
subnationalAIRules.sourceName: German Bundestag - EU AI Act implementation bill

governanceDomains.label: Frontier / GPAI
governanceDomains.description: General-purpose and frontier-model governance, including safety frameworks and GPAI obligations.

governanceDomains.label: Public sector
governanceDomains.description: Rules or guidance for government use, procurement, transparency, and public-service deployment.

governanceDomains.label: Employment / hiring
governanceDomains.description: Automated employment decision tools, hiring audits, and worker-facing AI disclosures.

governanceDomains.label: Biometric identification
governanceDomains.description: Remote biometric identification, biometric categorisation, and related high-risk uses.

governanceDomains.label: Synthetic media
governanceDomains.description: Deepfakes, generated content, provenance, watermarking, labelling, and notice-and-removal regimes.

governanceDomains.label: Healthcare
governanceDomains.description: AI systems deployed in medical, health, or care settings.

governanceDomains.label: Finance
governanceDomains.description: Financial-services AI governance, algorithmic risk, and consumer-facing financial decisions.

governanceDomains.label: Education / children
governanceDomains.description: AI uses affecting education, students, children, and youth.

governanceDomains.label: Defense / autonomous weapons
governanceDomains.description: Military AI, autonomous weapons, and defense-sector AI governance.

governanceDomains.label: Cybersecurity / critical infrastructure
governanceDomains.description: Security duties, critical infrastructure, resilience, and cyber-risk controls for AI systems.

governanceDomains.label: Compute / cloud / chips
governanceDomains.description: AI compute, hyperscale cloud, export controls, chips, and infrastructure bottlenecks.

governanceObligations.summary: The EU AI Act creates risk-based duties, including high-risk AI risk management and GPAI risk-management obligations under phased application.
governanceObligations.caveat: EU applicability is not the same as separate national enactment; member-state implementation and enforcement arrangements vary.
governanceObligations.verificationNotes: EUR-Lex confirms Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 and phased risk-based obligations. This obligation row is a workbench index into the official regulation, not a substitute for the legal text.

governanceObligations.summary: The EU AI Act includes transparency duties for specified AI systems and additional information duties for general-purpose AI model providers.
governanceObligations.caveat: Specific transparency duties depend on role, system type, and phased application date.

governanceObligations.summary: High-risk AI systems must satisfy conformity and quality-management requirements before placement on the EU market.
governanceObligations.caveat: Actual route depends on product sector, harmonized standards, notified bodies, and delegated implementation.

governanceObligations.summary: The EU AI Act prohibits specified AI practices, including certain manipulative, social-scoring, and biometric uses.
governanceObligations.caveat: Read the official legal text for exceptions, law-enforcement carve-outs, and phased applicability.

governanceObligations.summary: The CoE AI Convention requires parties to maintain measures addressing AI lifecycle risks and impacts on human rights, democracy, and rule of law.
governanceObligations.caveat: As of the snapshot, the Convention was not yet in force; treaty duties apply only for parties once entry-into-force conditions are met.
governanceObligations.sourceName: Council of Europe - Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence

governanceObligations.summary: Korea's AI Basic Act creates a national framework for high-impact AI and governance duties before its 2026 application date.
governanceObligations.caveat: Detailed obligations depend on subordinate rules and implementation guidance.

governanceObligations.summary: China's generative-AI measures require providers of public-facing generative AI services to satisfy filing, security, and content-governance expectations.
governanceObligations.caveat: This row summarizes the governance hook already tracked in the dataset; consult CAC sources for provider-specific filing details.
governanceObligations.sourceName: CAC - Interim Measures for Generative AI Services

governanceObligations.summary: China's AI rule stack includes generated-content governance and labelling expectations for public generative-AI services.
governanceObligations.caveat: Specific labelling obligations also interact with later synthetic-content measures not expanded in this row.
governanceObligations.sourceName: CAC - Interim Measures for Generative AI Services

governanceObligations.summary: California SB 53 requires covered frontier developers to publish safety frameworks.
governanceObligations.caveat: Coverage depends on statutory thresholds and the developer's connection to California.

governanceObligations.summary: California SB 53 requires covered frontier developers to report specified safety incidents.
governanceObligations.caveat: This is not a federal U.S. frontier-model law; scope is state-level and threshold-dependent.

governanceObligations.summary: The Seoul Frontier AI Safety Commitments ask participating companies to publish safety frameworks, thresholds, and severe-risk management processes.
governanceObligations.caveat: Company commitments are voluntary and should not be read as state law or treaty obligations.

governanceObligations.summary: The NIST GenAI Profile gives voluntary guidance for identifying, measuring, managing, and governing generative-AI risks.
governanceObligations.caveat: NIST guidance is influential technical guidance, not binding law by itself.
governanceObligations.sourceName: NIST - AI RMF Generative AI Profile

governanceObligations.summary: ISO/IEC 42001 provides AI management-system requirements for organizational governance across the AI lifecycle.
governanceObligations.caveat: ISO standards are voluntary unless adopted into contracts, procurement, or law.

governanceObligations.summary: NYC Local Law 144 requires annual independent bias audits for covered automated employment decision tools.
governanceObligations.caveat: The law is employment-specific and does not govern frontier models generally.
governanceObligations.sourceName: NYC DCWP - Automated Employment Decision Tools

governanceObligations.summary: The TAKE IT DOWN Act creates notice-and-removal duties for covered platforms handling nonconsensual intimate imagery, including AI-generated digital forgeries.
governanceObligations.caveat: This is a synthetic-media/content law, not broad frontier-model governance.

governanceObligations.summary: Australia's proposed mandatory guardrails would turn selected high-risk AI safety expectations into mandatory duties.
governanceObligations.caveat: This remains a proposal in the snapshot and must not be treated as binding law.
governanceObligations.sourceName: Department of Industry, Science and Resources - Proposed mandatory guardrails

governanceObligations.summary: Brazil's PL 2338/2023 would create a risk-based AI framework with duties for developers and operators.
governanceObligations.caveat: The bill remains proposed until enactment is verified through official sources.

governanceObligations.summary: Türkiye's draft AI law would create risk-assessment and compliance expectations for high-risk AI systems.
governanceObligations.caveat: The draft remains under legislative consideration and should not be treated as in force.
governanceObligations.sourceName: Grand National Assembly of Türkiye - Draft AI Law

governanceObligations.summary: Mexico's proposed federal AI law is tracked as a proposal for AI governance duties, including transparency-oriented obligations.
governanceObligations.caveat: Status is proposed and source pages are session-sensitive; do not treat as enacted law.

governanceObligations.summary: OpenAI's EU-facing general-purpose AI activity creates a conditional EU AI Act exposure hook captured in the lab exposure dataset.
governanceObligations.caveat: This is an applicability hook, not a public enforcement finding against the lab.

governanceObligations.summary: U.S. advanced-chip export controls are tracked as an infrastructure constraint affecting Chinese frontier-AI compute capacity.
governanceObligations.caveat: This is infrastructure governance context, not an AI-specific national law exposure row.

implementationMilestones.label: EU AI Act entered into force
implementationMilestones.summary: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 entered into force on 1 August 2024, with phased application after that date.

implementationMilestones.label: EU AI Act phased application
implementationMilestones.summary: The Act applies through phased deadlines; the next broad deadline tracked here is 2 August 2026.
implementationMilestones.verificationNotes: This milestone intentionally uses a broad next-deadline cue. Detailed article-by-article deadlines should be added as separate rows before legal reliance.

implementationMilestones.label: CoE AI Convention adopted; not yet in force in snapshot
implementationMilestones.summary: The Convention was adopted and opened for signature, but the snapshot notes that entry-into-force conditions had not yet been met.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: Council of Europe - Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence

implementationMilestones.label: Korea AI Basic Act adopted; application begins in 2026
implementationMilestones.summary: Korea's AI Basic Act is tracked as adopted with application beginning in 2026.

implementationMilestones.label: China generative-AI measures in force
implementationMilestones.summary: China's Interim Measures for generative-AI services are tracked as in force for public-facing generative-AI services.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: CAC - Interim Measures for Generative AI Services

implementationMilestones.label: California SB 53 signed
implementationMilestones.summary: California SB 53 is tracked as a binding state-level frontier-AI transparency law after signing.

implementationMilestones.label: Brazil PL 2338/2023 under consideration
implementationMilestones.summary: Brazil's AI framework bill remains tracked as proposed until enactment is verified.

implementationMilestones.label: Türkiye draft AI law under consideration
implementationMilestones.summary: Türkiye's draft AI law remains tracked as proposed pending official enactment.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: Grand National Assembly of Türkiye - Draft AI Law

implementationMilestones.label: Mexico federal AI proposal tracked
implementationMilestones.summary: Mexico's federal AI proposal is retained as proposed and not counted as binding law.

implementationMilestones.label: Norway AI Act implementation consultation
implementationMilestones.summary: Norway is tracked as proposed/implementation planning for EEA alignment with the EU AI Act.

infrastructureNodes.name: Advanced AI chips
infrastructureNodes.verificationNotes: NVIDIA primary source verifies H100 as a representative advanced AI GPU; broader chip ecosystem and bottleneck assessment are analytical synthesis.
infrastructureNodes.description: Leading-edge GPUs and AI accelerators (NVIDIA H100/B200, AMD MI300, Google TPU, custom ASICs from AWS, Meta, Microsoft, Apple). The single hardest bottleneck for frontier model training and inference. Concentrated supply means whoever controls fabrication, exports, and resale routes shapes who can build frontier systems at scale.
infrastructureNodes.scopeCaveat: Advanced AI chips are not themselves AI law — they are the material substrate that frontier AI governance constrains via export controls, procurement, and cloud allocation.

infrastructureNodes.name: Hyperscale cloud
infrastructureNodes.verificationNotes: Cloud-provider market structure is sourced from a secondary market-analysis provider and should be periodically refreshed.
infrastructureNodes.description: Large cloud providers — AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, Tencent Cloud — that host frontier model training and inference. Their allocation decisions, security baselines, and content moderation policies act as a de-facto governance layer. Provider-side controls (model gating, API rate-limits, jurisdictional routing) operate independently of any treaty.
infrastructureNodes.scopeCaveat: Cloud providers are not regulators; they are private actors whose decisions function as governance because they hold scarce capacity.

infrastructureNodes.name: U.S. BIS export controls on advanced computing ICs
infrastructureNodes.verificationNotes: BIS source verifies the 2025 AI-chip export-control action; this record remains out-of-scope as AI-specific law and is included as frontier-AI infrastructure governance.
infrastructureNodes.description: Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) export controls on advanced computing integrated circuits, gradually expanded since 2022 with country-by-country quota and licensing rules. Among the strongest geopolitical levers over frontier AI capacity — operates upstream of any AI-specific national law.
infrastructureNodes.scopeCaveat: Not an AI-specific instrument under the strict scope rule. Included here because doc1 (the seed brief) is explicit that this is one of the highest-leverage frontier-AI governance levers, and the assignment is about frontier-AI governance specifically.
infrastructureNodes.sourceName: U.S. BIS — Press release on AI chip controls

outOfScopeItems.name: EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: GDPR is a general data-protection regulation, not an AI-specific instrument. Its provisions affect AI systems but the law itself is not about AI governance.

outOfScopeItems.name: India Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP)
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: DPDP is a general data-protection statute. It is not an AI-specific instrument and is excluded from the main map data.

outOfScopeItems.name: U.S. BIS export controls on advanced AI chips
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: Export controls are powerful geopolitical levers but are not AI-governance instruments in the sense used here. They regulate trade in chips, not the development or deployment of AI systems.

outOfScopeItems.name: U.S.–Japan–Netherlands semiconductor export controls
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: Trilateral arrangement focused on semiconductor manufacturing equipment, not AI governance.

outOfScopeItems.name: Generic national cybersecurity laws
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: General cybersecurity statutes affect AI systems but are not AI-specific governance instruments.

outOfScopeItems.name: Generic national digital strategies
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: Broad digital strategies are excluded unless they include explicit AI governance provisions.

sourceNotes.note: This map tracks AI-specific governance instruments only. It excludes general privacy, export-control, chip, semiconductor, and cybersecurity instruments unless they are explicitly AI-specific.
The Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI is not yet in force as of 19 May 2026. Entry into force requires five ratifications, including at least three Council of Europe member states.

Coverage for UNESCO and UNGA instruments is represented via 'covered_by_membership' across UN member states. This does not imply per-country sign-on; it reflects the multilateral nature of those instruments.
ISO/IEC standards are voluntary international standards. There are no country signatures; participation is via the global standardization system.
Company commitments, GPAI Council outputs, and NIST technical guidance are shown as governance context. They do not create country-level signature, ratification, or binding-law rows in this dataset.
CEN-CENELEC AI Act standards work is standardization infrastructure. It should not be read as a separate national AI law or explicit state endorsement.
The Frontier Model Forum is non-state / industry governance, not an intergovernmental instrument. It is shown for context but has no country-level participation rows.
The Paris AI Action Summit Statement on Inclusive and Sustainable AI was notably not signed by the United States or the United Kingdom.
This dataset is a May 2026 snapshot. Treaty and signature status can change quickly. Always verify time-sensitive details against official sources before relying on them.

datasetReleases.title: May 2026 research snapshot
datasetReleases.summary: Baseline public snapshot covering countries, frontier labs, international instruments, national and subnational AI rules, typed lab exposure, source metadata, and evidence dossiers.
recordsAdded: Research presets, Table view, Methodology, correction links, lab exposure workbench, evidence dossiers, and source delta monitor.
recordsChanged: High- and medium-priority legal/source records reviewed and softened where official sources did not support strong claims.
recordsDowngradedOrRemoved: Low-confidence binding claims were excluded from strong binding-law map effects.
sourceCorrections: Official-source host classification unified and source-audit metadata warnings cleared.
unresolvedManualReview: Council of Europe Treaty Office blocks scripted delta-monitor fetches and requires browser/manual review for treaty participation changes.
